title: Nebukawa no sensei
wikitext:
[]: Nebukawa no sensei             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Apr 12, 2015 7:19 pm I searched around the net for a bit but didn't really find much info or talk on this guy. Not that I expected it, but still. Anyhow, is his name just a reference to the Nebukawa railway station? Also, I read on Hikari's wiki page that her name is taken from a train, so is it the same for Nebukawa except his name being taken from a station? I'm curious. Hikari (Light), Nozomi (Wish), and Kodama (Echo) are the names of the three train services of the Tokaido Shinkansen, the first Bullet Train line in Japan. The original trains were the Hikari service, the slower Kodama trains were introduced next, followed by the much faster Nozomi trains, the three Horaki sisters being named in the order of increasing speeds.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 12, 2015 8:42 pm Good question. I'm not even sure what the Kanji for his name - the Kanji for the station are "Root, Prefecture, River". While the station is a likely source for the name, it's also possible that "Nebukawa" is a legitimate (if uncommon) Japanese name and Anno picked it.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Apr 12, 2015 8:50 pm Eh? "Nebukawa no Sensei" is a fan name and a joke name based on one of his very few identifying details: Episode 3 script wrote: OLD TEACHER: I lived in Nebukawa at the time, which is underwater now...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 12, 2015 11:11 pm Nebukawa (根府川) appears to be an area in Odawara (in Kanagawa Prefecture, where NGE takes place), outlined here .

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Mon Apr 13, 2015 9:41 am Monk Ed wrote: Eh? "Nebukawa no Sensei" is a fan name and a joke name based on one of his very few identifying details:

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Apr 13, 2015 6:12 pm Monk Ed wrote: Eh? "Nebukawa no Sensei" is a fan name and a joke name based on one of his very few identifying details:

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Apr 13, 2015 8:07 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Funny thing, how this sensei and the other sensei in Shinji's life have no real names to go with them.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Apr 13, 2015 9:47 pm Of course I know that - but what I was commenting on was just how entirely Anno turned these two senseis in Shinji's life into total non-entities. Since "sensei" doesn't always have to mean "teacher" - it can mean "boss" or "big shot" in some contexts like when Toji uses it ("Oi, sansa!") so Shinji's "sensei" need not even have been a teacher. We don't even know the character's gender!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 17, 2015 12:27 am I'm pretty sure Shinji is using "sensei" in the traditional sense, rather than the "hey, look at me, I'm the token Kansaiben dude!" one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 17, 2015 2:13 pm Sachi wrote: TIf he had been staying with a family member, he would have probably been a lot more well-adjusted by the beginning of the series

[]: Nebukawa no sensei             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Apr 12, 2015 7:19 pm I searched around the net for a bit but didn't really find much info or talk on this guy. Also, I read on Hikari's wiki page that her name is taken from a train, so is it the same for Nebukawa except his name being taken from a station? I'm curious. Hikari (Light), Nozomi (Wish), and Kodama (Echo) are the names of the three train services of the Tokaido Shinkansen, the first Bullet Train line in Japan. The original trains were the Hikari service, the slower Kodama trains were introduced next, followed by the much faster Nozomi trains, the three Horaki sisters being named in the order of increasing speeds.

While the station is a likely source for the name, it's also possible that "Nebukawa" is a legitimate (if uncommon) Japanese name and Anno picked it.
